item.title: Daily Telegraph – ‘I lost the vast majority of work the moment I put my head above the parapet’
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Sharron Davies: ‘I lost the vast majority of work the moment I put my head above the parapet’ After Davies was robbed of a gold medal in 1980, the former Olympian is campaigning to keep biological men out of women’s sports categories ByJudith Woods Sharron Davies, Olympic swimmer, former Gladiator and sports commentator, is sitting…
item.content:encoded:
Sharron Davies: ‘I lost the vast majority of work the moment I put my head above the parapet’

After Davies was robbed of a gold medal in 1980, the former Olympian is campaigning to keep biological men out of women’s sports categories

ByJudith Woods

Sharron Davies, Olympic swimmer, former Gladiator and sports commentator, is sitting in the high-ceilinged splendour of her Georgian house on the outskirts of Bath. Elegant and tanned-and-toned in an All Saints sundress, dachshund Ed pottering by her feet and Flash, her blind Basset hound in the next room, it’s hard not to conclude that she is literally Living the Dream.

Read more at The Telegraph

item.title: Olympic legend Sharron Davies leads Nike boycott
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Nike used trans influencer Dylan Mulvaney – who identifies as a woman but is yet to have surgery – to show off the sports brand’s new female range, but Olympian Sharron Davies was left unimpressed Olympic swimming legend Sharron Davies has called for a boycott on sports clothing giant Nike after they used trans woman Dylan Mulvaney to…
item.content:encoded:
Nike used trans influencer Dylan Mulvaney – who identifies as a woman but is yet to have surgery – to show off the sports brand’s new female range, but Olympian Sharron Davies was left unimpressed

Olympic swimming legend Sharron Davies has called for a boycott on sports clothing giant Nike after they used trans woman Dylan Mulvaney to model their new female range.

The iconic sports brand hired influencer Mulvaney – who identifies as a woman but has not had surgery – in their latest shoot. In the pictures, the 26-year-old comedian and actor is seen posing in a Nike sports bra and leggings.

Read the full article at The Mirror online

item.title: Sharron Davies challenges trans athletes claims
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Sharron Davies, the Olympic swimming medallist, has condemned remarks made at a Holyrood committee where it was claimed there was no evidence to prove trans-identifying athletes pose a threat to women’s participation in sport. Heidi Vistisen, policy manager at Leap Sport Scotland, which promotes LGBTI participation in sport, made the remarks during a health committee…
item.content:encoded:
Sharron Davies, the Olympic swimming medallist, has condemned remarks made at a Holyrood committee where it was claimed there was no evidence to prove trans-identifying athletes pose a threat to women’s participation in sport.

Heidi Vistisen, policy manager at Leap Sport Scotland, which promotes LGBTI participation in sport, made the remarks during a health committee session where she insisted no proof existed that biological males got an unfair advantage when competing in women’s sporting events.

Read full article at The Times

item.title: Sharron on GB News
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Feminist groups have expressed shock after it was revealed that a Parkrun women’s record is still held by trans-athlete and criminal, Lauren Jeska. But should transgender women be excluded because of their physical advantage or are they being treated unfairly? Former Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies believes the gender category you perform in, should be based…
item.content:encoded:
Feminist groups have expressed shock after it was revealed that a Parkrun women’s record is still held by trans-athlete and criminal, Lauren Jeska.

But should transgender women be excluded because of their physical advantage or are they being treated unfairly?

Former Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies believes the gender category you perform in, should be based on the biological reality of your gender, Rebecca Reid says trans people should be able to compete in non-contact sports.

item.title: In praise of Sharron Davies
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: It’s been quite a while since we celebrated any of Sharron Davies’s considerable achievements in the pool – well, a bronze and a silver in 1990 at Auckland was the last time – but I would bet a box full of brand-new Speedos to a secondhand pair of goggles that nothing has made her prouder…
item.content:encoded:
It’s been quite a while since we celebrated any of Sharron Davies’s considerable achievements in the pool – well, a bronze and a silver in 1990 at Auckland was the last time – but I would bet a box full of brand-new Speedos to a secondhand pair of goggles that nothing has made her prouder than her part in World Athletics’ decision to ban transgender women from competing in female international events. She has campaigned for this for years with great courage and at considerable cost.

Without her energy and bravery, it is possible that Seb Coe could have fudged the solution announced last week, when he ruled that he was following swimming and rugby rather than cycling and rowing, and there was no fair way to include transgender athletes in elite women’s sport. And thank heaven for that. ‘I’m so pleased Seb has protected female athletes around the world at long last. It’s been a struggle though,’ Davies said afterwards. ‘Sport is about safety and fairness, then inclusion – in that order.’ You couldn’t put it better.

The full story is at The Spectator

item.title: Thank you for standing up for female athletes
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: ‘Thank you for standing up for female athletes’: Ex-Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies praises World Athletics for voting to exclude transgender athletes from the women’s category Ex-swimmer Sharron Davies said the move shows woman are worthy of fair sport Former British Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has come out in support of World Athletics’ decision to exclude transgender…
item.content:encoded:
‘Thank you for standing up for female athletes’: Ex-Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies praises World Athletics for voting to exclude transgender athletes from the women’s category

  • Ex-swimmer Sharron Davies said the move shows woman are worthy of fair sport

Former British Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has come out in support of World Athletics’ decision to exclude transgender athletes from women’s category events – and thanked the organisation for ‘standing up for female athletes’.

Under new rules announced by Sebastian Coe, the governing body’s president, transgender women will be banned from competing in the female category at international athletics events in order to ‘prioritise fairness and the integrity of the female competition before inclusion’.

It comes as a result of what Lord Coe told BBC Radio 4 was a clear consensus ‘from the vast majority of the core stakeholders we have’, including female athletes, coaches, and member federations.

The full story at the Mail Online

item.title: Transgender women banned by World Athletics
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer hails decision to ban transgender women from competing in female international events Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has hailed the landmark decision by World Athletics to ban transgender women from competing in female international events. The former medallist, 60, said she was “quite emotional” to hear the decision after campaigning for five years on…
item.content:encoded:
Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer hails decision to ban transgender women from competing in female international events

Plymouth-born Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has hailed the landmark decision by World Athletics to ban transgender women from competing in female international events.

The former medallist, 60, said she was “quite emotional” to hear the decision after campaigning for five years on the issue. She revealed last year that she faced “financial ruin” after being subjected to horrific abuse after arguing that transgender athletes had a genetic advantage over female competitors who were born female.

Read the story at Plymouth Live

item.title: Sharron Davies MBE is an Ambassador for Arthrosamid
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: We’re delighted to announce that ex-Olympic swimmer, world-renowned broadcaster and former Gladiator, Sharron Davies MBE has joined Contura Orthopaedics as an Ambassador for Arthrosamid®. Sharron is a wonderful advocate for the beneﬁts of an active, balanced lifestyle – combining her love of exercise, with a busy family life. In her own ﬁtness regime, as well…
item.content:encoded:
We’re delighted to announce that ex-Olympic swimmer, world-renowned broadcaster and former Gladiator, Sharron Davies MBE has joined Contura Orthopaedics as an Ambassador for Arthrosamid®.

Sharron is a wonderful advocate for the beneﬁts of an active, balanced lifestyle – combining her love of exercise, with a busy family life. In her own ﬁtness regime, as well as working with clients online and one-to-one, Sharron promotes exercise programmes that encourage mobility, strength and conﬁdence.

Naturally, Sharron, who has just turned 60, has encountered injuries along the way and feels that her earlier intensive training has certainly taken its toll on her joints. Over the years, the stiffness and pain in her right knee has increased to grade 4 arthritis, especially after snapping her ACL and reconstruction surgery whilst ﬁlming Gladiators. Frustratingly, it aﬀects her ability to exercise comfortably and enjoy her day-to-day activities.

Read more here >

item.title: The Battle Against Unfair Play In Women’s Sport
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: Sharron Davies, longtime advocate and campaigner for women in sport and justice in the face of the consequences of cheating and discrimination endured by female athletes, is the co-author of a new book on the theme with this writer, Craig Lord. Due out in April 2023, Unfair Play: The Battle For Women’s Sports (pre-order available) will be published…
item.content:encoded:
Sharron Davies, longtime advocate and campaigner for women in sport and justice in the face of the consequences of cheating and discrimination endured by female athletes, is the co-author of a new book on the theme with this writer, Craig Lord.

Due out in April 2023, Unfair Play: The Battle For Women’s Sports (pre-order available) will be published by Forum Press.

Editorial director George Owers bought world rights to Unfair Play: The Battle for Women’s Sports from Caroline Hardman at Hardman & Swainson.

item.title: Scottish MSPs in trans talks ‘don’t care about women’s sport
item.dc:creator: Sharron Davies
item.description: The former Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has accused Scottish ministers of bias in their efforts to extend trans rights by speaking only “to people who back up their point of view”. Davies said SNP ministers were like the “men in suits” of the International Olympic Committee and “do not care about women’s sports in the…
item.content:encoded:
The former Olympic swimmer Sharron Davies has accused Scottish ministers of bias in their efforts to extend trans rights by speaking only “to people who back up their point of view”.

Davies said SNP ministers were like the “men in suits” of the International Olympic Committee and “do not care about women’s sports in the slightest — it is heartbreaking, when half the world is female”.

She spoke up after MSPs considering the impact of reforms to the Gender Recognition Act heard from officials at sportscotland and Leap Sport Scotland, who were accused of failing to represent women’s interests by selectively quoting official guidance on transgender inclusion in sport.

Davies, 59, told The Times the Olympic committee and the Scottish government…

View the full story here
